task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: 15-6-024 Measure media step accuracy in High Quality mode (4_pass) [Special Tests]

section.title: Function
section.p: This test measures the media step accuracy.

li.p: Media is calibrated just before executing the test. Also applicable for service media ! (via UI)
Media cut before starting the test.
Media transport calibration has been performed (verify via the calibration overview of ORS)

section.title: Description
section.p: This test measures the media step accuracy.
The test will measure the media step performance and display the relevant values. If the test finishes successful, it does not mean that the media step accuracy is within specification.
To judge the values, verify if the values of tabs named:
“Segment AverageErrors” should be between +/- 33 um among all 6 segments.

If one of the values is out of the above boundaries, first verify that the preconditions were met before starting the test! If this was the case, the 15 Escalation media step out of specification procedure should be followed.
In case the media step accuracy test is within specification an acceptable “high quality print mode” print quality should be achieved under normal conditions.
For detailed information: see 15 Extended explanation SDS test 15-6-024 Media step accuracy.
